free_text: #doi:10.1103/PhysRevC.20.88
Excitation functions for radioactive isotopes produced
by proton bombardment of Fe, Co, and W in the energy
range from 10 to 60 MeV
The 30 inch scattering chamber at the
76 inch Isochr. Cyclotron at the Univ. of Cal., Davis
Incident proton beam energy 63.0+-0.3 MeV
determined by neutron time-of-night measurements.
Conservative value of 1% for energy spread and
uncertainty of incident beam energy chosen to allow
for foil thickness uncertainty and energy loss
straggling effects.
6000 micro-Coulombs of protons as measured
using Faraday cup.
The gamma-ray spectrum of each thin foil was
recorded with a PGT large crystal Ge(Li) spectrometer
system (13.8% efficiency relative to
7.62 cm x 7.62cm NaI(TI) crystal).
Correction factor of 0.71 was applied to the
originally reported data.
Corrected for:
. proton loss at increasing depths in target stack due
to inelastic scattering.
. attenuation (5% at 20 MeV; 1% at 60 MeV).
Accuracy of Faraday cup total deposited charge
measurement 5%.
Each 1.27 cm square foil was measured
with a precision micrometer to determine the
average foil thickness in the central region to an
accuracy of better than +-5%.
Uncertainty in in standard gamma-ray sources
(1.17%), resulted in efficiency curve errors of 5 %
at 100 KeV, and 0.2% at 800 KeV.
Peak stripping errors 3%.
Foils placed beyond the range showed that
secondary neutrons contributed less than 1.0% to the
measured isotope activities and therefore did not
represent a significant source of error.
EN-RSL - A conservative value of +-1% for the energy
spread and uncertainty of the incident beam was chosen
to allow for foil thickness uncertainty and energy
loss straggling effects.
Data error (no description in the article)
Energy error (no description in the article)
Data were received from author through
publisher (PAPS PRVCA-20-88-4)
CAJaD.
SD. In Subent 10 SF5: M+ -> (M). BIB was
upgraded. Cosmetic changes in all Subents. Subent 011
was split in four (011-014). Digitized data in all
Subents were replaced by data from author.
Subent 4 deleted - Authors didn't include them to the
final tables results. (From the article: '...two points
for 58Co could be a result of recoils from cobalt
foils or neutron effects'
OS. Correction in sub.7
